items.title: Gesture-Controlled Performance System for Slow Music
items.category: GESTURE INTERFACES • LIVE A/V • EMBODIED SYSTEMS
items.description: A modular gesture-driven audiovisual system where the body becomes the instrument—G.C.P.S.S.M. (Gesture-Controlled Performance System for Slow Music) extends it into released audio.
items.fullDescription: MediaPipe and TouchDesigner map body movement to real-time sound and generative visuals. First shown at Fever Dream; since then it has served as a modular live A/V platform—gestures drive glitch and drone textures, pulses, and projections—with an intentionally slow, embodied approach to control and coding. G.C.P.S.S.M. (Gesture-Controlled Performance System for Slow Music) is the slow-music branch of this system: gesture and speed control rework older material as samples, released on asymmetrica.
items.medium: Gesture interfaces, real-time sound/visual generation, sensor-based interaction, ambient performance systems
items.technical: MediaPipe, TouchDesigner, Python, real-time audio synthesis, custom control pipelines
items.themes: Embodied computation, glitch aesthetics, slow music, gesture-controlled performance, poetic control systems
